Misconfigurations.Title: Web App uses latest TLS version
Misconfigurations.Description: Use a more recent TLS/SSL policy for the App Service
Misconfigurations.Message: App service does not require a secure TLS version.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: The TLS version being outdated and has known vulnerabilities

Misconfigurations.Title: The minimum TLS version for Storage Accounts should be TLS1_2 or higher
Misconfigurations.Description:
Azure Storage supports four versions of the TLS protocol: 1.0, 1.1, 1.2, and 1.3.
Azure Storage uses TLS 1.2 or TLS 1.3 on public HTTPS endpoints, while TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 are still supported for backward compatibility.
This check will warn if the minimum TLS version is set lower than TLS1_2. TLS1_2 and TLS1_3 are both allowed.
Misconfigurations.Message: Storage account uses an insecure TLS version.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use a more recent TLS version for the storage account

Misconfigurations.Title: Storage account should have secure transfer and minimum TLS version configured
Misconfigurations.Description:
Storage accounts should enforce HTTPS for secure data transfer and use a minimum TLS version of 1.2 or higher.
This ensures that logs and data stored in the storage account are encrypted in transit.
Azure Storage encrypts all data at rest by default, but transport encryption is also critical.
Misconfigurations.Message: Storage account does not use a secure TLS version.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable secure transfer and set minimum TLS version to TLS1_2 or higher

Misconfigurations.Title: The default action on Storage account network rules should be set to deny
Misconfigurations.Description:
The default_action for network rules should come into effect when no other rules are matched.
The default action should be set to Deny.
Misconfigurations.Message: A network rule is configured to allow access.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set network rules to deny

Misconfigurations.Title: Key vault should have the network acl block specified
Misconfigurations.Description:
Network ACLs allow you to reduce your exposure to risk by limiting what can access your key vault.

The default action of the Network ACL should be set to deny for when IPs are not matched. Azure services can be allowed to bypass.
Misconfigurations.Message: Vault network ACL does not block access by default.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set a network ACL for the key vault

Misconfigurations.Title: Key vault should have the network acl block specified
Misconfigurations.Description:
Network ACLs allow you to reduce your exposure to risk by limiting what can access your key vault.

The default action of the Network ACL should be set to deny for when IPs are not matched. Azure services can be allowed to bypass.
Misconfigurations.Message: Vault network ACL does not block access by default.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set a network ACL for the key vault

Misconfigurations.Title: The default action on Storage account network rules should be set to deny
Misconfigurations.Description:
The default_action for network rules should come into effect when no other rules are matched.
The default action should be set to Deny.
Misconfigurations.Message: A network rule is configured to allow access.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set network rules to deny

Misconfigurations.Title: Ensure AKS has an API Server Authorized IP Ranges enabled
Misconfigurations.Description: The API server is the central way to interact with and manage a cluster. To improve cluster security and minimize attacks, the API server should only be accessible from a limited set of IP address ranges.
Misconfigurations.Message: Cluster does not limit API access to specific IP addresses.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Limit the access to the API server to a limited IP range

Misconfigurations.Title: Ensure AKS cluster has Network Policy configured
Misconfigurations.Description: The Kubernetes object type NetworkPolicy should be defined to have opportunity allow or block traffic to pods, as in a Kubernetes cluster configured with default settings, all pods can discover and communicate with each other without any restrictions.
Misconfigurations.Message: Kubernetes cluster does not have a network policy set.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Configure a network policy

Misconfigurations.Title: Enable disk encryption on managed disk
Misconfigurations.Description: Manage disks should be encrypted at rest. When specifying the encryption_settings block, the enabled attribute should be set to true.
Misconfigurations.Message: Managed disk is not encrypted.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable encryption on managed disks

Misconfigurations.Title: The minimum TLS version for Storage Accounts should be TLS1_2 or higher
Misconfigurations.Description:
Azure Storage supports four versions of the TLS protocol: 1.0, 1.1, 1.2, and 1.3.
Azure Storage uses TLS 1.2 or TLS 1.3 on public HTTPS endpoints, while TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 are still supported for backward compatibility.
This check will warn if the minimum TLS version is set lower than TLS1_2. TLS1_2 and TLS1_3 are both allowed.
Misconfigurations.Message: Storage account uses an insecure TLS version.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use a more recent TLS version for the storage account

Misconfigurations.Title: Storage account should have secure transfer and minimum TLS version configured
Misconfigurations.Description:
Storage accounts should enforce HTTPS for secure data transfer and use a minimum TLS version of 1.2 or higher.
This ensures that logs and data stored in the storage account are encrypted in transit.
Azure Storage encrypts all data at rest by default, but transport encryption is also critical.
Misconfigurations.Message: Storage account does not use a secure TLS version.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable secure transfer and set minimum TLS version to TLS1_2 or higher

Misconfigurations.Title: Ensure the Function App can only be accessed via HTTPS. The default is false.
Misconfigurations.Description: By default, clients can connect to function endpoints by using both HTTP or HTTPS. You should redirect HTTP to HTTPs because HTTPS uses the SSL/TLS protocol to provide a secure connection, which is both encrypted and authenticated.
Misconfigurations.Message: Function app does not have HTTPS enforced.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: You can redirect all HTTP requests to the HTTPS port.

Misconfigurations.Title: A security group rule should not allow unrestricted ingress from any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Description: Opening up ports to allow connections from the public internet is generally to be avoided. You should restrict access to IP addresses or ranges that are explicitly required where possible.
Misconfigurations.Message: Security group rule allows unrestricted ingress from any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set a more restrictive cidr range

Misconfigurations.Title: The minimum TLS version for Storage Accounts should be TLS1_2 or higher
Misconfigurations.Description:
Azure Storage supports four versions of the TLS protocol: 1.0, 1.1, 1.2, and 1.3.
Azure Storage uses TLS 1.2 or TLS 1.3 on public HTTPS endpoints, while TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 are still supported for backward compatibility.
This check will warn if the minimum TLS version is set lower than TLS1_2. TLS1_2 and TLS1_3 are both allowed.
Misconfigurations.Message: Storage account uses an insecure TLS version.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use a more recent TLS version for the storage account

Misconfigurations.Title: The minimum TLS version for Storage Accounts should be TLS1_2 or higher
Misconfigurations.Description:
Azure Storage supports four versions of the TLS protocol: 1.0, 1.1, 1.2, and 1.3.
Azure Storage uses TLS 1.2 or TLS 1.3 on public HTTPS endpoints, while TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 are still supported for backward compatibility.
This check will warn if the minimum TLS version is set lower than TLS1_2. TLS1_2 and TLS1_3 are both allowed.
Misconfigurations.Message: Storage account uses an insecure TLS version.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use a more recent TLS version for the storage account

Misconfigurations.Title: The default action on Storage account network rules should be set to deny
Misconfigurations.Description:
The default_action for network rules should come into effect when no other rules are matched.
The default action should be set to Deny.
Misconfigurations.Message: A network rule is configured to allow access.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set network rules to deny

Misconfigurations.Title: The default action on Storage account network rules should be set to deny
Misconfigurations.Description:
The default_action for network rules should come into effect when no other rules are matched.
The default action should be set to Deny.
Misconfigurations.Message: A network rule is configured to allow access.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set network rules to deny

Misconfigurations.Title: Storage account should have secure transfer and minimum TLS version configured
Misconfigurations.Description:
Storage accounts should enforce HTTPS for secure data transfer and use a minimum TLS version of 1.2 or higher.
This ensures that logs and data stored in the storage account are encrypted in transit.
Azure Storage encrypts all data at rest by default, but transport encryption is also critical.
Misconfigurations.Message: Storage account does not use a secure TLS version.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable secure transfer and set minimum TLS version to TLS1_2 or higher

Misconfigurations.Title: Storage account should have secure transfer and minimum TLS version configured
Misconfigurations.Description:
Storage accounts should enforce HTTPS for secure data transfer and use a minimum TLS version of 1.2 or higher.
This ensures that logs and data stored in the storage account are encrypted in transit.
Azure Storage encrypts all data at rest by default, but transport encryption is also critical.
Misconfigurations.Message: Storage account does not use a secure TLS version.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable secure transfer and set minimum TLS version to TLS1_2 or higher

Misconfigurations.Title: Security Contact Disabled
Misconfigurations.Description:
If a security contact is disabled, critical alerts from Microsoft Defender for Cloud will not be delivered to that contact, potentially causing security incidents to go unnoticed.

Security contacts should be enabled to ensure that designated personnel receive timely notifications about security threats and incidents.
Misconfigurations.Message: Security contact is disabled and will not receive notifications.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable the security contact to ensure security notifications are delivered.

Misconfigurations.Title: Trusted Microsoft Services should have bypass access to Storage accounts
Misconfigurations.Description:
Some Microsoft services that interact with storage accounts operate from networks that can't be granted access through network rules.
To help this type of service work as intended, allow the set of trusted Microsoft services to bypass the network rules
Misconfigurations.Message: Network rules do not allow bypass for Microsoft Services.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Allow Trusted Microsoft Services to bypass

Misconfigurations.Title: The minimum TLS version for Storage Accounts should be TLS1_2 or higher
Misconfigurations.Description:
Azure Storage supports four versions of the TLS protocol: 1.0, 1.1, 1.2, and 1.3.
Azure Storage uses TLS 1.2 or TLS 1.3 on public HTTPS endpoints, while TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 are still supported for backward compatibility.
This check will warn if the minimum TLS version is set lower than TLS1_2. TLS1_2 and TLS1_3 are both allowed.
Misconfigurations.Message: Storage account uses an insecure TLS version.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use a more recent TLS version for the storage account

Misconfigurations.Title: Storage account should have secure transfer and minimum TLS version configured
Misconfigurations.Description:
Storage accounts should enforce HTTPS for secure data transfer and use a minimum TLS version of 1.2 or higher.
This ensures that logs and data stored in the storage account are encrypted in transit.
Azure Storage encrypts all data at rest by default, but transport encryption is also critical.
Misconfigurations.Message: Storage account does not use a secure TLS version.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable secure transfer and set minimum TLS version to TLS1_2 or higher

Misconfigurations.Title: Launch configuration with unencrypted block device.
Misconfigurations.Description: Block devices should be encrypted to ensure sensitive data is held securely at rest.
Misconfigurations.Message: EBS block device is not encrypted.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Turn on encryption for all block devices

Misconfigurations.Title: Config configuration aggregator should be using all regions for source
Misconfigurations.Description:
Sources that aren't covered by the aggregator are not include in the configuration. The configuration aggregator should be configured with all_regions for the source.
This will help limit the risk of any unmonitored configuration in regions that are thought to be unused.
Misconfigurations.Message: Configuration aggregation is not set to source from all regions.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set the aggregator to cover all regions

Misconfigurations.Title: Config configuration aggregator should be using all regions for source
Misconfigurations.Description:
Sources that aren't covered by the aggregator are not include in the configuration. The configuration aggregator should be configured with all_regions for the source.
This will help limit the risk of any unmonitored configuration in regions that are thought to be unused.
Misconfigurations.Message: Configuration aggregation is not set to source from all regions.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set the aggregator to cover all regions

Misconfigurations.Title: EBS volumes must be encrypted
Misconfigurations.Description: By enabling encryption on EBS volumes you protect the volume, the disk I/O and any derived snapshots from compromise if intercepted.
Misconfigurations.Message: EBS volume is not encrypted.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable encryption of EBS volumes

Misconfigurations.Title: aws_instance should activate session tokens for Instance Metadata Service.
Misconfigurations.Description: IMDS v2 (Instance Metadata Service) introduced session authentication tokens which improve security when talking to IMDS. By default aws_instance resource sets IMDS session auth tokens to be optional. To fully protect IMDS you need to enable session tokens by using metadata_options block and its http_tokens variable set to required.
Misconfigurations.Message: Instance does not require IMDS access to require a token.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable HTTP token requirement for IMDS

Misconfigurations.Title: Instance with unencrypted block device.
Misconfigurations.Description: Block devices should be encrypted to ensure sensitive data is held securely at rest.
Misconfigurations.Message: Root block device is not encrypted.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Turn on encryption for all block devices

Misconfigurations.Title: A security group rule should not allow unrestricted egress to any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Description: Opening up ports to connect out to the public internet is generally to be avoided. You should restrict access to IP addresses or ranges that are explicitly required where possible.
Misconfigurations.Message: Security group rule allows unrestricted egress to any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set a more restrictive cidr range

Misconfigurations.Title: A security group rule should not allow unrestricted egress to any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Description: Opening up ports to connect out to the public internet is generally to be avoided. You should restrict access to IP addresses or ranges that are explicitly required where possible.
Misconfigurations.Message: Security group rule allows unrestricted egress to any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set a more restrictive cidr range

Lines.Content: Description: Allow http to client host

Misconfigurations.Title: A security group rule should not allow unrestricted egress to any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Description: Opening up ports to connect out to the public internet is generally to be avoided. You should restrict access to IP addresses or ranges that are explicitly required where possible.
Misconfigurations.Message: Security group rule allows unrestricted egress to any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set a more restrictive cidr range

Lines.Content: Description: Allow http to client host

Misconfigurations.Title: A security group rule should not allow unrestricted egress to any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Description: Opening up ports to connect out to the public internet is generally to be avoided. You should restrict access to IP addresses or ranges that are explicitly required where possible.
Misconfigurations.Message: Security group rule allows unrestricted egress to any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set a more restrictive cidr range

Lines.Content: Description: Allow http to client host

Misconfigurations.Title: Security groups should not allow unrestricted ingress to SSH or RDP from any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Description:
Security groups provide stateful filtering of ingress and egress network traffic to AWS
resources. It is recommended that no security group allows unrestricted ingress access to
remote server administration ports, such as SSH to port 22 and RDP to port 3389.
Misconfigurations.Message: Security group rule allows unrestricted ingress from any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set a more restrictive CIDR range

Lines.Content: Description: Allow http to client host

Misconfigurations.Title: ECR repository has image scans disabled.
Misconfigurations.Description: Repository image scans should be enabled to ensure vulnerable software can be discovered and remediated as soon as possible.
Misconfigurations.Message: Image scanning is not enabled
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable ECR image scanning

Misconfigurations.Title: ECR images tags shouldn't be mutable.
Misconfigurations.Description: ECR images should be set to IMMUTABLE to prevent code injection through image mutation. This can be done by setting image_tag_mutability to IMMUTABLE
Misconfigurations.Message: Repository tags are mutable.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Only use immutable images in ECR

Misconfigurations.Title: ECS Task Definitions with EFS volumes should use in-transit encryption
Misconfigurations.Description: ECS task definitions that have volumes using EFS configuration should explicitly enable in transit encryption to prevent the risk of data loss due to interception.
Misconfigurations.Message: Task definition includes a volume which does not have in-transit-encryption enabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable in transit encryption when using efs

Misconfigurations.Title: ECS Task Definitions with EFS volumes should use in-transit encryption
Misconfigurations.Description: ECS task definitions that have volumes using EFS configuration should explicitly enable in transit encryption to prevent the risk of data loss due to interception.
Misconfigurations.Message: Task definition includes a volume which does not have in-transit-encryption enabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable in transit encryption when using efs

Misconfigurations.Title: Task definition defines sensitive environment variable(s).
Misconfigurations.Description: You should not make secrets available to a user in plaintext in any scenario. Secrets can instead be pulled from a secure secret storage system by the service requiring them.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container definition contains a potentially sensitive in environment variable name "{\"endline\": 29, \"explicit\": true, \"filepath\": \"cft/ecs/ecs-task-definition/deploy.yml\", \"fskey\": \"eb9b502123e0764495f1d7d6757db3062ea8fa30d861707b494501a9cae21b24\", \"managed\": true, \"parent\": {\"endline\": 29, \"explicit\": false, \"filepath\": \"cft/ecs/ecs-task-definition/deploy.yml\", \"fskey\": \"eb9b502123e0764495f1d7d6757db3062ea8fa30d861707b494501a9cae21b24\", \"managed\": true, \"resource\": \"taskdefinition\", \"sourceprefix\": \"\", \"startline\": 29, \"unresolvable\": false}, \"resource\": \"\", \"sourceprefix\": \"\", \"startline\": 29, \"unresolvable\": false, \"value\": \"PASSWORD\"}"
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use secrets for the task definition

Misconfigurations.Title: EKS should have the encryption of secrets enabled
Misconfigurations.Description: EKS cluster resources should have the encryption_config block set with protection of the secrets resource.
Misconfigurations.Message: Cluster does not have secret encryption enabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable encryption of EKS secrets

Misconfigurations.Title: EKS Clusters should have the public access disabled
Misconfigurations.Description: EKS clusters are available publicly by default, this should be explicitly disabled in the vpc_config of the EKS cluster resource.
Misconfigurations.Message: Public cluster access is enabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Don't enable public access to EKS Clusters

Misconfigurations.Title: Elasticache Replication Group uses unencrypted traffic.
Misconfigurations.Description: Traffic flowing between Elasticache replication nodes should be encrypted to ensure sensitive data is kept private.
Misconfigurations.Message: Replication group does not have transit encryption enabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable in transit encryption for replication group

Misconfigurations.Title: Kinesis stream is unencrypted.
Misconfigurations.Description: Kinesis streams should be encrypted to ensure sensitive data is kept private. Additionally, non-default KMS keys should be used so granularity of access control can be ensured.
Misconfigurations.Message: Stream does not use KMS encryption.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable in transit encryption

Misconfigurations.Title: CloudFront distribution does not have a WAF in front.
Misconfigurations.Description: You should configure a Web Application Firewall in front of your CloudFront distribution. This will mitigate many types of attacks on your web application.
Misconfigurations.Message: Distribution does not utilize a WAF.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable WAF for the CloudFront distribution

Misconfigurations.Title: CloudFront distribution uses outdated SSL/TLS protocols.
Misconfigurations.Description:
You should not use outdated/insecure TLS versions for encryption. You should be using the latest TLS policies available.
Note: that setting *minimum_protocol_version = "TLSv1.X"* is only possible when *cloudfront_default_certificate* is false (eg. you are not using the cloudfront.net domain name).
If *cloudfront_default_certificate* is true then the Cloudfront API will only allow setting *minimum_protocol_version = "TLSv1"*, and setting it to any other value will result in a perpetual diff in your *terraform plan*'s.
The only option when using the cloudfront.net domain name is to ignore this rule.
Misconfigurations.Message: Distribution uses an insecure minimum TLS protocol version.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use the most modern TLS/SSL policies available

Misconfigurations.Title: CloudTrail should use Customer managed keys to encrypt the logs
Misconfigurations.Description: Using AWS managed keys does not allow for fine grained control.  Using Customer managed keys provides comprehensive control over cryptographic keys, enabling management of policies, permissions, and rotation, thus enhancing security and compliance measures for sensitive data and systems.
Misconfigurations.Message: CloudTrail does not use a customer managed key to encrypt the logs.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use Customer managed key

Misconfigurations.Title: Cloudtrail log validation should be enabled to prevent tampering of log data
Misconfigurations.Description: Illicit activity could be removed from the logs. Log validation should be activated on Cloudtrail logs to prevent the tampering of the underlying data in the S3 bucket. It is feasible that a rogue actor compromising an AWS account might want to modify the log data to remove trace of their actions.
Misconfigurations.Message: Trail does not have log validation enabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Turn on log validation for Cloudtrail

Misconfigurations.Title: DAX Cluster should always encrypt data at rest
Misconfigurations.Description: Data can be freely read if compromised. Amazon DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX) encryption at rest provides an additional layer of data protection by helping secure your data from unauthorized access to the underlying storage.
Misconfigurations.Message: DAX encryption is not enabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable encryption at rest for DAX Cluster

Misconfigurations.Title: DocumentDB storage must be encrypted
Misconfigurations.Description: Unencrypted sensitive data is vulnerable to compromise. Encryption of the underlying storage used by DocumentDB ensures that if their is compromise of the disks, the data is still protected.
Misconfigurations.Message: Cluster storage does not have encryption enabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable storage encryption

Misconfigurations.Title: ECR repository has image scans disabled.
Misconfigurations.Description: Repository image scans should be enabled to ensure vulnerable software can be discovered and remediated as soon as possible.
Misconfigurations.Message: Image scanning is not enabled
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable ECR image scanning

Misconfigurations.Title: ECR images tags shouldn't be mutable.
Misconfigurations.Description: ECR images should be set to IMMUTABLE to prevent code injection through image mutation. This can be done by setting image_tag_mutability to IMMUTABLE
Misconfigurations.Message: Repository tags are mutable.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Only use immutable images in ECR

Misconfigurations.Title: EFS Encryption has not been enabled
Misconfigurations.Description: If your organization is subject to corporate or regulatory policies that require encryption of data and metadata at rest, we recommend creating a file system that is encrypted at rest, and mounting your file system using encryption of data in transit.
Misconfigurations.Message: File system is not encrypted.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable encryption for EFS

Misconfigurations.Title: Elasticsearch domain uses plaintext traffic for node to node communication.
Misconfigurations.Description: Traffic flowing between Elasticsearch nodes should be encrypted to ensure sensitive data is kept private.
Misconfigurations.Message: Domain does not have in-transit encryption enabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable encrypted node to node communication

Misconfigurations.Title: Elasticsearch doesn't enforce HTTPS traffic.
Misconfigurations.Description:
Plain HTTP is unencrypted and human-readable. This means that if a malicious actor was to eavesdrop on your connection, they would be able to see all of your data flowing back and forth.
You should use HTTPS, which is HTTP over an encrypted (TLS) connection, meaning eavesdroppers cannot read your traffic.
Misconfigurations.Message: Domain does not enforce HTTPS.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enforce the use of HTTPS for ElasticSearch

Misconfigurations.Title: Elasticsearch domain isn't encrypted at rest.
Misconfigurations.Description: You should ensure your Elasticsearch data is encrypted at rest to help prevent sensitive information from being read by unauthorised users.
Misconfigurations.Message: Domain does not have at-rest encryption enabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable ElasticSearch domain encryption

Misconfigurations.Title: Elasticsearch domain endpoint is using outdated TLS policy.
Misconfigurations.Description: You should not use outdated/insecure TLS versions for encryption. You should be using TLS v1.2+.
Misconfigurations.Message: Domain does not have a secure TLS policy.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use the most modern TLS/SSL policies available

Misconfigurations.Title: Ensure MQ Broker is not publicly exposed
Misconfigurations.Description: Public access of the MQ broker should be disabled and only allow routes to applications that require access.
Misconfigurations.Message: Broker has public access enabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Disable public access when not required

Misconfigurations.Title: Neptune storage must be encrypted at rest
Misconfigurations.Description: Encryption of Neptune storage ensures that if their is compromise of the disks, the data is still protected.
Misconfigurations.Message: Cluster does not have storage encryption enabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable encryption of Neptune storage

Misconfigurations.Title: Redshift clusters should use at rest encryption
Misconfigurations.Description: Redshift clusters that contain sensitive data or are subject to regulation should be encrypted at rest to prevent data leakage should the infrastructure be compromised.
Misconfigurations.Message: Cluster does not use a customer managed encryption key.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable encryption using CMK

Misconfigurations.Title: Redshift clusters should use at rest encryption
Misconfigurations.Description: Redshift clusters that contain sensitive data or are subject to regulation should be encrypted at rest to prevent data leakage should the infrastructure be compromised.
Misconfigurations.Message: Cluster does not have encryption enabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable encryption using CMK

Misconfigurations.Title: Redshift cluster should be deployed into a specific VPC
Misconfigurations.Description:
Redshift clusters that are created without subnet details will be created in EC2 classic mode, meaning that they will be outside of a known VPC and running in tenant.
In order to benefit from the additional security features achieved with using an owned VPC, the subnet should be set.
Misconfigurations.Message: Cluster is deployed outside of a VPC.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Deploy Redshift cluster into a non default VPC

Misconfigurations.Title: Redshift cluster should be deployed into a specific VPC
Misconfigurations.Description:
Redshift clusters that are created without subnet details will be created in EC2 classic mode, meaning that they will be outside of a known VPC and running in tenant.
In order to benefit from the additional security features achieved with using an owned VPC, the subnet should be set.
Misconfigurations.Message: Cluster is deployed outside of a VPC.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Deploy Redshift cluster into a non default VPC

Misconfigurations.Title: Redshift cluster should be deployed into a specific VPC
Misconfigurations.Description:
Redshift clusters that are created without subnet details will be created in EC2 classic mode, meaning that they will be outside of a known VPC and running in tenant.
In order to benefit from the additional security features achieved with using an owned VPC, the subnet should be set.
Misconfigurations.Message: Cluster is deployed outside of a VPC.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Deploy Redshift cluster into a non default VPC

Misconfigurations.Title: Redshift cluster should be deployed into a specific VPC
Misconfigurations.Description:
Redshift clusters that are created without subnet details will be created in EC2 classic mode, meaning that they will be outside of a known VPC and running in tenant.
In order to benefit from the additional security features achieved with using an owned VPC, the subnet should be set.
Misconfigurations.Message: Cluster is deployed outside of a VPC.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Deploy Redshift cluster into a non default VPC

Misconfigurations.Title: S3 Access block should block public policy
Misconfigurations.Description: S3 bucket policy should have block public policy to prevent users from putting a policy that enable public access.
Misconfigurations.Message: Public access block does not block public policies
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Prevent policies that allow public access being PUT

Misconfigurations.Title: S3 Access Block should Ignore Public ACL
Misconfigurations.Description: S3 buckets should ignore public ACLs on buckets and any objects they contain. By ignoring rather than blocking, PUT calls with public ACLs will still be applied but the ACL will be ignored.
Misconfigurations.Message: Public access block does not ignore public ACLs
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable ignoring the application of public ACLs in PUT calls

Misconfigurations.Title: S3 Access block should restrict public bucket to limit access
Misconfigurations.Description: S3 buckets should restrict public policies for the bucket. By enabling, the restrict_public_buckets, only the bucket owner and AWS Services can access if it has a public policy.
Misconfigurations.Message: Public access block does not restrict public buckets
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Limit the access to public buckets to only the owner or AWS Services (eg; CloudFront)

Misconfigurations.Title: S3 Access block should block public ACL
Misconfigurations.Description: S3 buckets should block public ACLs on buckets and any objects they contain. By blocking, PUTs with fail if the object has any public ACL a.
Misconfigurations.Message: No public access block so not blocking public acls
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable blocking any PUT calls with a public ACL specified

Misconfigurations.Title: S3 Access block should block public policy
Misconfigurations.Description: S3 bucket policy should have block public policy to prevent users from putting a policy that enable public access.
Misconfigurations.Message: No public access block so not blocking public policies
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Prevent policies that allow public access being PUT

Misconfigurations.Title: S3 Access Block should Ignore Public ACL
Misconfigurations.Description: S3 buckets should ignore public ACLs on buckets and any objects they contain. By ignoring rather than blocking, PUT calls with public ACLs will still be applied but the ACL will be ignored.
Misconfigurations.Message: No public access block so not blocking public acls
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable ignoring the application of public ACLs in PUT calls

Misconfigurations.Title: S3 Access block should restrict public bucket to limit access
Misconfigurations.Description: S3 buckets should restrict public policies for the bucket. By enabling, the restrict_public_buckets, only the bucket owner and AWS Services can access if it has a public policy.
Misconfigurations.Message: No public access block so not restricting public buckets
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Limit the access to public buckets to only the owner or AWS Services (eg; CloudFront)

Misconfigurations.Title: S3 encryption should use Customer Managed Keys
Misconfigurations.Description:
Encryption using AWS keys provides protection for your S3 buckets. To gain greater control over encryption, such as key rotation, access policies, and auditability, use customer managed keys (CMKs) with SSE-KMS.
Note that SSE-KMS is not supported for S3 server access logging destination buckets; in such cases, use SSE-S3 instead.
Misconfigurations.Message: Bucket does not encrypt data with a customer managed key.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use SSE-KMS with a customer managed key (CMK)

Misconfigurations.Title: Root and user volumes on Workspaces should be encrypted
Misconfigurations.Description: Workspace volumes for both user and root should be encrypted to protect the data stored on them.
Misconfigurations.Message: Root volume does not have encryption enabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Root and user volume encryption should be enabled

Misconfigurations.Title: Root and user volumes on Workspaces should be encrypted
Misconfigurations.Description: Workspace volumes for both user and root should be encrypted to protect the data stored on them.
Misconfigurations.Message: User volume does not have encryption enabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Root and user volume encryption should be enabled

Misconfigurations.Title: RDS encryption has not been enabled at a DB Instance level.
Misconfigurations.Description:
Encryption should be enabled for an RDS Database instances.
When enabling encryption by setting the kms_key_id.
Misconfigurations.Message: Instance does not have storage encryption enabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable encryption for RDS instances

Misconfigurations.Title: RDS Publicly Accessible
Misconfigurations.Description: Ensures RDS instances and RDS Cluster instances are not launched into the public cloud.
Misconfigurations.Message: Instance has Public Access enabled
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Remove the public endpoint from the RDS instance.

Misconfigurations.Title: There is no encryption specified or encryption is disabled on the RDS Cluster.
Misconfigurations.Description:
Encryption should be enabled for an RDS Aurora cluster.
When enabling encryption by setting the kms_key_id, the storage_encrypted must also be set to true.
Misconfigurations.Message: Cluster does not specify a customer managed key for storage encryption.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable encryption for RDS clusters

Misconfigurations.Title: There is no encryption specified or encryption is disabled on the RDS Cluster.
Misconfigurations.Description:
Encryption should be enabled for an RDS Aurora cluster.
When enabling encryption by setting the kms_key_id, the storage_encrypted must also be set to true.
Misconfigurations.Message: Cluster does not have storage encryption enabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable encryption for RDS clusters

Misconfigurations.Title: Unencrypted SNS topic.
Misconfigurations.Description: Topics should be encrypted to protect their contents.
Misconfigurations.Message: Topic does not have encryption enabled.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Turn on SNS Topic encryption

Misconfigurations.Title: Unencrypted SQS queue.
Misconfigurations.Description: Queues should be encrypted to protect queue contents.
Misconfigurations.Message: Queue is not encrypted
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Turn on SQS Queue encryption

Misconfigurations.Title: aws_instance should activate session tokens for Instance Metadata Service.
Misconfigurations.Description: IMDS v2 (Instance Metadata Service) introduced session authentication tokens which improve security when talking to IMDS. By default aws_instance resource sets IMDS session auth tokens to be optional. To fully protect IMDS you need to enable session tokens by using metadata_options block and its http_tokens variable set to required.
Misconfigurations.Message: Instance does not require IMDS access to require a token.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable HTTP token requirement for IMDS

Misconfigurations.Title: Instance with unencrypted block device.
Misconfigurations.Description: Block devices should be encrypted to ensure sensitive data is held securely at rest.
Misconfigurations.Message: Root block device is not encrypted.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Turn on encryption for all block devices

Misconfigurations.Title: aws_instance should activate session tokens for Instance Metadata Service.
Misconfigurations.Description: IMDS v2 (Instance Metadata Service) introduced session authentication tokens which improve security when talking to IMDS. By default aws_instance resource sets IMDS session auth tokens to be optional. To fully protect IMDS you need to enable session tokens by using metadata_options block and its http_tokens variable set to required.
Misconfigurations.Message: Instance does not require IMDS access to require a token.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable HTTP token requirement for IMDS

Misconfigurations.Title: Instance with unencrypted block device.
Misconfigurations.Description: Block devices should be encrypted to ensure sensitive data is held securely at rest.
Misconfigurations.Message: Root block device is not encrypted.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Turn on encryption for all block devices

Misconfigurations.Title: Load balancers should drop invalid headers
Misconfigurations.Description:
Passing unknown or invalid headers through to the target poses a potential risk of compromise.
By setting drop_invalid_header_fields to true, anything that does not conform to well known, defined headers will be removed by the load balancer.
Misconfigurations.Message: Application load balancer is not set to drop invalid headers.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set drop_invalid_header_fields to true

Misconfigurations.Title: Load balancer is exposed to the internet.
Misconfigurations.Description: There are many scenarios in which you would want to expose a load balancer to the wider internet, but this check exists as a warning to prevent accidental exposure of internal assets. You should ensure that this resource should be exposed publicly.
Misconfigurations.Message: Load balancer is exposed publicly.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Switch to an internal load balancer or add a tfsec ignore

Misconfigurations.Title: Use of plain HTTP.
Misconfigurations.Description:
Plain HTTP is unencrypted and human-readable. This means that if a malicious actor was to eavesdrop on your connection, they would be able to see all of your data flowing back and forth.
You should use HTTPS, which is HTTP over an encrypted (TLS) connection, meaning eavesdroppers cannot read your traffic.
Misconfigurations.Message: Listener for application load balancer does not use HTTPS.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Switch to HTTPS to benefit from TLS security features

Misconfigurations.Title: A security group rule should not allow unrestricted egress to any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Description: Opening up ports to connect out to the public internet is generally to be avoided. You should restrict access to IP addresses or ranges that are explicitly required where possible.
Misconfigurations.Message: Security group rule allows unrestricted egress to any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set a more restrictive cidr range

Misconfigurations.Title: A security group rule should not allow unrestricted egress to any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Description: Opening up ports to connect out to the public internet is generally to be avoided. You should restrict access to IP addresses or ranges that are explicitly required where possible.
Misconfigurations.Message: Security group rule allows unrestricted egress to any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set a more restrictive cidr range

Misconfigurations.Title: Instances in a subnet should not receive a public IP address by default.
Misconfigurations.Description: You should limit the provision of public IP addresses for resources. Resources should not be exposed on the public internet, but should have access limited to consumers required for the function of your application.
Misconfigurations.Message: Subnet associates public IP address.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set the instance to not be publicly accessible

Misconfigurations.Title: Instances in a subnet should not receive a public IP address by default.
Misconfigurations.Description: You should limit the provision of public IP addresses for resources. Resources should not be exposed on the public internet, but should have access limited to consumers required for the function of your application.
Misconfigurations.Message: Subnet associates public IP address.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set the instance to not be publicly accessible

Misconfigurations.Title: S3 Access block should block public ACL
Misconfigurations.Description: S3 buckets should block public ACLs on buckets and any objects they contain. By blocking, PUTs with fail if the object has any public ACL a.
Misconfigurations.Message: No public access block so not blocking public acls
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable blocking any PUT calls with a public ACL specified

Misconfigurations.Title: S3 Access block should block public ACL
Misconfigurations.Description: S3 buckets should block public ACLs on buckets and any objects they contain. By blocking, PUTs with fail if the object has any public ACL a.
Misconfigurations.Message: Public access block does not block public ACLs
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable blocking any PUT calls with a public ACL specified

Misconfigurations.Title: S3 Access block should block public policy
Misconfigurations.Description: S3 bucket policy should have block public policy to prevent users from putting a policy that enable public access.
Misconfigurations.Message: No public access block so not blocking public policies
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Prevent policies that allow public access being PUT

Misconfigurations.Title: S3 Access block should block public policy
Misconfigurations.Description: S3 bucket policy should have block public policy to prevent users from putting a policy that enable public access.
Misconfigurations.Message: Public access block does not block public policies
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Prevent policies that allow public access being PUT

Misconfigurations.Title: S3 Access Block should Ignore Public ACL
Misconfigurations.Description: S3 buckets should ignore public ACLs on buckets and any objects they contain. By ignoring rather than blocking, PUT calls with public ACLs will still be applied but the ACL will be ignored.
Misconfigurations.Message: No public access block so not blocking public acls
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable ignoring the application of public ACLs in PUT calls

Misconfigurations.Title: S3 Access Block should Ignore Public ACL
Misconfigurations.Description: S3 buckets should ignore public ACLs on buckets and any objects they contain. By ignoring rather than blocking, PUT calls with public ACLs will still be applied but the ACL will be ignored.
Misconfigurations.Message: Public access block does not ignore public ACLs
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable ignoring the application of public ACLs in PUT calls

Misconfigurations.Title: S3 Access block should restrict public bucket to limit access
Misconfigurations.Description: S3 buckets should restrict public policies for the bucket. By enabling, the restrict_public_buckets, only the bucket owner and AWS Services can access if it has a public policy.
Misconfigurations.Message: No public access block so not restricting public buckets
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Limit the access to public buckets to only the owner or AWS Services (eg; CloudFront)

Misconfigurations.Title: S3 Access block should restrict public bucket to limit access
Misconfigurations.Description: S3 buckets should restrict public policies for the bucket. By enabling, the restrict_public_buckets, only the bucket owner and AWS Services can access if it has a public policy.
Misconfigurations.Message: Public access block does not restrict public buckets
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Limit the access to public buckets to only the owner or AWS Services (eg; CloudFront)

Misconfigurations.Title: A security group rule should not allow unrestricted egress to any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Description: Opening up ports to connect out to the public internet is generally to be avoided. You should restrict access to IP addresses or ranges that are explicitly required where possible.
Misconfigurations.Message: Security group rule allows unrestricted egress to any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set a more restrictive cidr range

Misconfigurations.Title: S3 encryption should use Customer Managed Keys
Misconfigurations.Description:
Encryption using AWS keys provides protection for your S3 buckets. To gain greater control over encryption, such as key rotation, access policies, and auditability, use customer managed keys (CMKs) with SSE-KMS.
Note that SSE-KMS is not supported for S3 server access logging destination buckets; in such cases, use SSE-S3 instead.
Misconfigurations.Message: Bucket does not encrypt data with a customer managed key.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use SSE-KMS with a customer managed key (CMK)

Misconfigurations.Title: S3 encryption should use Customer Managed Keys
Misconfigurations.Description:
Encryption using AWS keys provides protection for your S3 buckets. To gain greater control over encryption, such as key rotation, access policies, and auditability, use customer managed keys (CMKs) with SSE-KMS.
Note that SSE-KMS is not supported for S3 server access logging destination buckets; in such cases, use SSE-S3 instead.
Misconfigurations.Message: Bucket does not encrypt data with a customer managed key.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use SSE-KMS with a customer managed key (CMK)

Misconfigurations.Title: aws_instance should activate session tokens for Instance Metadata Service.
Misconfigurations.Description: IMDS v2 (Instance Metadata Service) introduced session authentication tokens which improve security when talking to IMDS. By default aws_instance resource sets IMDS session auth tokens to be optional. To fully protect IMDS you need to enable session tokens by using metadata_options block and its http_tokens variable set to required.
Misconfigurations.Message: Instance does not require IMDS access to require a token.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable HTTP token requirement for IMDS

Misconfigurations.Title: aws_instance should activate session tokens for Instance Metadata Service.
Misconfigurations.Description: IMDS v2 (Instance Metadata Service) introduced session authentication tokens which improve security when talking to IMDS. By default aws_instance resource sets IMDS session auth tokens to be optional. To fully protect IMDS you need to enable session tokens by using metadata_options block and its http_tokens variable set to required.
Misconfigurations.Message: Instance does not require IMDS access to require a token.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Enable HTTP token requirement for IMDS

Misconfigurations.Title: A security group rule should not allow unrestricted egress to any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Description: Opening up ports to connect out to the public internet is generally to be avoided. You should restrict access to IP addresses or ranges that are explicitly required where possible.
Misconfigurations.Message: Security group rule allows unrestricted egress to any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set a more restrictive cidr range

Misconfigurations.Title: A security group rule should not allow unrestricted egress to any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Description: Opening up ports to connect out to the public internet is generally to be avoided. You should restrict access to IP addresses or ranges that are explicitly required where possible.
Misconfigurations.Message: Security group rule allows unrestricted egress to any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set a more restrictive cidr range

Misconfigurations.Title: Security groups should not allow unrestricted ingress to SSH or RDP from any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Description:
Security groups provide stateful filtering of ingress and egress network traffic to AWS
resources. It is recommended that no security group allows unrestricted ingress access to
remote server administration ports, such as SSH to port 22 and RDP to port 3389.
Misconfigurations.Message: Security group rule allows unrestricted ingress from any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set a more restrictive CIDR range

Misconfigurations.Title: Security groups should not allow unrestricted ingress to SSH or RDP from any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Description:
Security groups provide stateful filtering of ingress and egress network traffic to AWS
resources. It is recommended that no security group allows unrestricted ingress access to
remote server administration ports, such as SSH to port 22 and RDP to port 3389.
Misconfigurations.Message: Security group rule allows unrestricted ingress from any IP address.
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set a more restrictive CIDR range
